quickFacts: Italy's chocolate capital — home of Nutella and gianduja
First capital of unified Italy, much less crowded than Rome or Milan
Aperitivo culture rivals Milan — generous free buffets with your spritz
The Turin Shroud is only displayed on special occasions

places.name: Mole Antonelliana
places.caption: Turin's iconic skyline symbol
places.description: Take the panoramic glass elevator to the top for 360° views of the Alps and city. Houses the excellent National Cinema Museum inside.

places.name: Piazza Castello
places.caption: Grand heart of baroque Turin
places.description: The city's main square, surrounded by porticoed walkways, Palazzo Madama, and the Royal Palace. Perfect for an evening passeggiata.

places.name: Quadrilatero Romano
places.caption: Ancient Roman quarter turned foodie paradise
places.description: Turin's oldest neighbourhood is packed with aperitivo bars, vintage shops, and buzzing restaurants. The Saturday street market is unmissable.

places.name: Egyptian Museum
places.caption: Second-largest Egyptian collection in the world
places.description: Rivals the Cairo museum with stunning artefacts, mummy galleries, and a beautifully redesigned interior. Allow at least 2 hours.

places.name: Parco del Valentino
places.caption: Riverside park for lazy afternoons
places.description: Stroll along the Po River, rent a bike, or picnic under the trees. Don't miss the Medieval Village, a life-size replica of a Piedmontese hamlet.

places.name: Superga Basilica
places.caption: Hilltop church with jaw-dropping panorama
places.description: Take the historic rack railway up the hill for the best view of Turin backed by the entire Alpine arc. Sunset here is pure magic.

places.name: Porta Palazzo Market
places.caption: Europe's largest open-air market
places.description: A sensory overload of fresh produce, cheeses, street food, and multicultural vibes. Saturday mornings are the liveliest — grab a porchetta panino.

places.name: San Salvario
places.caption: Multicultural neighbourhood & nightlife hub
places.description: Turin's most diverse area is packed with ethnic eateries, craft cocktail bars, and a young creative crowd. The place to be after dark.

places.name: Reggia di Venaria
places.caption: Italy's answer to Versailles
places.description: A stunning Savoy royal palace just outside the city with grand halls, contemporary art exhibitions, and 60 hectares of manicured gardens.

places.name: Monte dei Cappuccini
places.caption: Sunset viewpoint over the city
places.description: A short climb across the Po River to this hilltop church rewards you with the best free panorama of Turin's skyline and the snow-capped Alps.

places.name: Royal Palace (Palazzo Reale)
places.caption: Savoy residence
places.description: UNESCO-listed former royal palace with lavish state rooms, the Royal Armoury, Royal Library (Leonardo's self-portrait) and gardens.

places.name: Piazza San Carlo
places.caption: Turin's drawing room
places.description: Elegant Baroque square with twin churches, the equestrian statue of Duke Emanuele Filiberto and historic caffès beneath arcades.

places.name: Caffè Culture
places.caption: Birthplace of Italian coffee
places.description: Bicerin (coffee + chocolate + cream) at Caffè Al Bicerin since 1763. Turin invented gianduja chocolate and vermouth too.

places.name: Sacra di San Michele
places.caption: Mountaintop abbey
places.description: Romanesque abbey perched on a mountain 40 km from Turin. Inspired The Name of the Rose. Dramatic approach stairway carved from rock.

places.name: Eataly Turin
places.caption: Where it all started
places.description: The original Eataly opened in a former vermouth factory in 2007. Five floors of Italian food, restaurants and cooking classes.

foodScene: Turin invented gianduja (chocolate-hazelnut), so try it everywhere. Bicerin is the city's legendary hot drink — espresso, chocolate, and cream layered in a glass. Bagna cauda (warm anchovy-garlic dip) is the classic Piedmontese starter. Aperitivo is a religion here — order a drink at any bar and graze the free buffets.
